content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    :   File                                                   Date  :  May 6, 1978

                                                                           A78-05-050

From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Gina S-K

Subject:  Agenda item at opinion request meeting of March 17, 1978


Must political contributions made by partner s who discuss same in
advance be aggregated for the purpose of determining when a commit-
tee threshold has been reached?

No.  As long as the contributions are truly independent and all
that is involved is a discussion of what issues or candidates will
be most helpful to the business and therefore should be supported.

State of California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants

Date : March 16, 1978

Gina S-K

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject: Contributions by members of partnership.

Under a partnership's rules, the partnership may not
make political contributions. Partners do, however,
get together and perhaps 4 out of the 10 partners make
individual contributions to a single candidate or
ballot measure. They discuss the fact that they are
making the contributions. Must the partner's contributions
to aggregated purposes of determining when a committee
threshold has been passed? Are they a recipient or
had a
[illegible]
committee

sections.question: Must political contributions made by partners who discuss same in advance be aggregated for the purpose of determining when a committee threshold has been reached?
sections.conclusion: No. As long as the contributions are truly independent and all that is involved is a discussion of what issues or candidates will be most helpful to the business and therefore should be supported.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether individual political contributions by business partners who discuss their contributions in advance must be aggregated to determine committee formation thresholds under campaign finance law.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Individual partner contributions need not be aggregated for committee threshold purposes if the contributions are truly independent, even though the partners discuss which candidates or issues to support.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two memoranda addressing the same substantive question. The second memo from March 16, 1978 provides more detailed question context, while the May 6, 1978 memo provides the FPPC's concise answer. Both are integrated into the response. Document is from 1978 with limited formatting, but core Q/A sections are clearly identifiable.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must political contributions made by partners who discuss same in advance be aggregated for the purpose of determining when a committee threshold has been reached?

CONCLUSION: No. As long as the contributions are truly independent and all that is involved is a discussion of what issues or candidates will be most helpful to the business and therefore should be supported.
embedding.first_500_words: **Memorandum** To: File Date: May 6, 1978 From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Gina S-K Subject: Agenda item at opinion request meeting of March 17, 1978 Must political contributions made by partners who discuss same in advance be aggregated for the purpose of determining when a committee threshold has been reached? No. As long as the contributions are truly independent and all that is involved is a discussion of what issues or candidates will be most helpful to the business and therefore should be supported. State of California Memorandum To: Opinion Request Meeting Participants Date: March 16, 1978 Gina S-K From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Subject: Contributions by members of partnership. Under a partnership's rules, the partnership may not make political contributions. Partners do, however, get together and perhaps 4 out of the 10 partners make individual contributions to a single candidate or ballot measure. They discuss the fact that they are making the contributions. Must the partner's contributions be aggregated purposes of determining when a committee threshold has been reached? Are they a recipient or major donor committee? NO - not a committee
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter addressing whether contributions by multiple business partners discussing their political giving in advance must be aggregated for campaign finance committee threshold purposes. The Commission concluded that aggregation is not required if contributions remain independent despite discussion among partners.
